# Zoom Vendor Review

**Review Date:** 2024-09-18  
**Owner:** Finance / IT  
**Classification:** Confidential

## Plan & Usage

- **Plan:** Zoom Business+
- **Licensed hosts:** 520
- **Active hosts (30-day):** 448
- **Rooms licensed:** 22 conference rooms
- **Webinars add-on:** 5 licenses (Marketing team)

## Annual Cost Breakdown

| Line Item             | Annual Cost |
|-----------------------|-------------|
| Business+ (520 hosts) | $104,000    |
| Zoom Rooms (22)       | $26,400     |
| Webinar add-on (5)    | $9,750      |
| Phone (selected users)| $18,200     |
| **Total**             | **$158,350**|

## Benchmarking

Microsoft Teams is included in our M365 E3 license. If we migrated video to Teams:
- Estimated savings: $62,000–$75,000/year
- Migration effort: Medium (hardware recertification for Rooms, user training)
- User satisfaction risk: High — engineering strongly prefers Zoom UX

This conversation comes up every renewal. Engineering leadership has blocked Teams migration twice. Finance will push again at next exec QBR.

## Contract Notes

- **Renewal:** December 2024 — this is urgent
- Zoom offered 8% discount for 2-year commitment
- We counter-proposed 12% — currently in negotiation
- Minimum acceptable outcome: hold at current per-host rate ($200/host/yr)

## Idle License Issue

72 hosts (14%) have not hosted a meeting in 60+ days. IT should deprovision these before the December renewal to reduce committed seats.

## Risks

- Zoom's pivot to "AI Companion" features adds cost pressure in future tiers
- Zoom Phone is niche usage — only 62 employees use it; evaluating whether to cut and use mobile stipend instead

## Action Items

- [ ] IT to deprovision 72 idle hosts by 2024-11-30
- [ ] Finance to finalize 2-year vs 1-year model by 2024-11-15
- [ ] Exec QBR agenda item: Teams migration decision — scheduled 2025-Q1
